# torc — C++ Package Manager

## What is torc?
A minimal, Make-native C++ dependency manager. It fetches, builds, and installs
C++ dependencies, then generates includable .mak fragments.

## Commands
- `torc install` — fetch, SHA-256 verify, build, install packages
- `torc generate` — emit extdep.mak and localdep.mak
- `torc build` — compile sources directly (no Makefile needed)
- `torc compdb` — generate compile_commands.json for IDEs
- `torc update` — check for newer versions (--apply to rewrite manifest)
- `torc clean` — remove stale versions from depdir
- `torc list` — show declared packages
- `torc new <name>` — scaffold a new C++ project
- `torc init` — generate a starter Makefile
- `torc hook` — inject torc block into existing Makefile

Run `torc <command> --help` for options.

## Manifest Format (torc.yaml)
```yaml
depdir: ~/.local/share/torc
parallel: 4
ldlibs: -lpthread

packages:
  - name: fmt
    version: 10.1.1
    source: https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt/archive/refs/tags/10.1.1.tar.gz
    sha256: abc123...
    build: |
      cmake -B build -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=${PREFIX}
      cmake --build build -j${JOBS}
      cmake --install build
    discover: ./checkers/fmt.discover

checkers:
  - ./checkers/gitlab

toolchains:
  aarch64:
    cxx: aarch64-linux-gnu-g++
    cxxflags: --sysroot=/usr/aarch64-linux-gnu
    out: build-aarch64

local:
  libs:
    - name: util
      dir: lib/util
      include: lib/util/include
    - name: core
      dir: lib/core
      deps: [util]
  targets:
    - name: myapp
      dir: src
      deps: [core, util]
```

## Key Concepts
- `depdir` — shared directory where packages are installed (user or group level)
- `${PREFIX}` — substituted with install path during build
- `${JOBS}` — substituted with parallel job count
- `discover` — script that outputs transitive deps as YAML list
- `checkers` — scripts for version checking non-GitHub sources
- `toolchains` — named cross-compilation configurations
- `local` — inter-library dependency graph for multi-dir projects

## Checker Protocol
A checker script responds to:
- `<script> --can-check <url>` → exit 0 if it handles this URL
- `<script> --latest <url>` → print latest version to stdout

## Discover Protocol
A discover script receives: `<script> <name> <version> <prefix>`
Outputs: YAML list of additional package entries.

## Build Behavior
- `torc build` discovers *.cpp in src/, compiles with -MMD for incremental rebuilds
- Uses manifest packages for -I and -L flags automatically
- `--release` enables -O2 -DNDEBUG, default is -O0 -g
- `--toolchain=NAME` selects cross-compilation config from manifest

## Environment
- `CXX` — override compiler (default: g++)
- `NO_COLOR` — disable colored output
- Exit codes follow sysexits.h (64=usage, 65=data, 66=noinput, etc.)
